text: Dredging finds keep surfacing along the North Sea coast. A medieval wreck is not an obstruction to be logged and removed. The record under that mud is finite, and once it is gone it is gone.

Site NF-7 off Husum: hull timbers consistent with late 14th century, pending the radiocarbon dates. Cautious optimism. The Wadden Sea preserves wood remarkably well when the sediment stays undisturbed.
Maritime heritage and port operations need not be in opposition, but the burden of proof should not always fall on the wreck. Proper survey before dredging is not a delay. It is due diligence.
Three days of poor visibility on site, then one clear morning over the wreck makes up for all of it. The North Sea gives field seasons grudgingly. You learn to take the window when it opens.
